title: $regex
text: $regex Convert a string to a regex string. $regex ( String {, Flags }) Example: if (NAME1 = $regex("dog", "i")... Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description String String String to convert to a regex string. Flags String Regex flags to apply: i Return Values Returns the regex string if successful, or an empty string if an error occurred. In this case, $procerror contains a negative value identifying the exact error. Table 2. Values of $procerror Commonly Returned Following $regex Value Error constant Meaning -1014 <UPROCERR_REGEXSTRING> The regex string is not valid. -1110 <UPROCERR_TOPIC> The topic is not valid. -1301 <UPROCERR_SYNTAX > Syntax error. Use Allowed in all component types. Description A regex string is a group of characters and regex codes enclosed in slash marks ( / ) that can be used to match a string with a particular regex pattern. If the argument Flags is omitted the regex string can only match a case-sensitive string. For more information, see Regex Strings for Pattern Matching . $regex converts String to a regex string. Typically, the regex string is then used in logical expressions (such as if , repeat , or while statements) to check whether a string matches the particular regex pattern. Flags Flags determine how characters and regex codes are treated when performing a pattern-matching search. Table 3. Regex String Flags Flags Meaning Example Resulting Regex String Matches i Case-insensitive search $regex(“dog”, “i”) /dog/i dog Dog DOG Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
